hero.tagline: Design 360° wrap art for cups, mugs, and bottles — right in your browser.

custom.name: Name
custom.summary: Bottom Ø {bottomD} mm · top Ø {topD} mm · circumference {cMin}–{cMax} mm · artboard {w} × {h} mm

errors.name: Enter a name.
errors.height: Height must be a positive number of millimeters.
errors.dimensionMissing: Enter a diameter or a circumference.
errors.dimensionBoth: Enter either a diameter or a circumference, not both.
errors.dimensionPositive: Diameter and circumference must be positive numbers.
errors.engraveOrder: The engrave zone top must be above its bottom.
errors.engraveRange: The engrave zone must fit within the vessel height.

viewer.webglUnavailable: WebGL isn't available in this browser, so the 3D preview can't start.

import.empty: No importable shapes found in that SVG.

export.title: Export for your laser

lightburn.name: LightBurn
lightburn.notes: File → Import, then enable the rotary under Tools → Rotary Setup and enter the object diameter.

xtool.name: xTool Creative Space
xtool.notes: Import the SVG into XCS, choose cylindrical (rotary) processing and enter the object diameter.

lasergrbl.name: LaserGRBL
lasergrbl.notes: Open the SVG, enable the rotary axis and set steps-per-rotation from your machine's documentation.

generic.name: Generic SVG
generic.notes: Physical size is true millimeters — import at 100% scale and set up your rotary manually.

export.flattenShapesHint: Converts rects, ellipses and polygons to SVG paths for simpler importers.
export.embedMetadataHint: Adds a comment with the object diameter and setup notes to the file.

warnings.textFontsLightburn: Text stays as live text — LightBurn needs the same font installed, or convert text to paths first.
warnings.textFonts: Text stays as live text — the receiving program needs the same font installed.
warnings.imagesEmbedded: Raster images are embedded as data URIs, which can make the SVG large.
warnings.rasterFonts: Text is rendered with this device's fonts into the PNG.
warnings.transparentBg: Transparent background: some laser programs render transparency as black.

export.rotaryReminder: Rotary reminder: enter object diameter {diameter} mm in your laser software.
export.projectDownloadHint: A .lasergen.json backup you can re-import in laser-gen.

rotary.dpiNote: SVG exports are true millimeters — import at 100% scale. Raster exports embed their DPI.
rotary.step1: Open Tools → Rotary Setup and enable the rotary.
rotary.step2: Chuck rotary: enter steps-per-rotation from your machine docs. Roller rotary: enter your roller diameter.
rotary.step3: Enter the object diameter above as the rotary's object diameter.

vectorize.title: Vectorize
vectorize.noSource: Import an image onto the artboard or upload one to trace.

photo.title: Photo prep
photo.selectHint: Select an image on the artboard — or add one — to prepare it for engraving.
photo.tooManyDots: Too many dots ({count}) — increase the cell size.
photo.resolutionNote: Processing is capped at {px} px on the long edge.

powderCoatedSteel.name: Powder-coated steel
powderCoatedSteel.note: The laser burns the coating away to bright metal, so dark tones stay dark (no invert). Extra contrast and Floyd–Steinberg keep tones crisp.

bareStainless.name: Bare stainless
bareStainless.note: Marking darkens bare steel; a mild contrast boost with Floyd–Steinberg keeps gradients smooth.

wood.name: Wood
wood.note: Wood burns dark and bleeds a little; softer contrast and an ordered 8×8 dither avoid muddy, over-burned patches.

glass.name: Glass
glass.note: Glass etches bright where the beam hits, so the image is inverted; Floyd–Steinberg keeps the frosting detailed.

coatedCeramic.name: Coated ceramic
coatedCeramic.note: Like powder coat, the glaze burns to bright; moderate contrast with Floyd–Steinberg suits coated mugs and tiles.

library.importMergePrompt: Merge with your existing library? Press OK to merge, Cancel to replace everything.
library.importError: That file isn't a valid laser-gen library export.
library.emptyHint: Design a wrap in the studio, then save it to your library with the save button in the toolbar.

jobs.title: Job tracker
jobs.notePlaceholder: How did it burn? Settings, issues, results…

assets.title: Assets
assets.emptyHint: Save art here to reuse it across projects.

ai.title: AI assistant
ai.noProvider: The AI assistant needs a provider. Add your own API key in Settings — it never leaves this device except to the provider you choose.
ai.generatingHint: Asking the model for SVG art — this can take a few seconds.

svg.title: Prompt to SVG
svg.placeholder: A leaping fox, geometric line art…
svg.failed: The model did not return usable SVG.

image.title: Prompt to image
image.vectorizeHint: The image is on the artboard — run Vectorize on it for engraving-ready paths.

copilot.title: Design copilot
copilot.hint: Ask about layout, engraving suitability, or let it tweak the design.

ai.title: AI providers
ai.label: Name
ai.baseUrlHint: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint: Ollama, LM Studio, OpenRouter, vLLM… Must allow browser requests (CORS).
ai.errorBaseUrl: A base URL is required for custom endpoints.
ai.errorKey: An API key is required for this provider.
ai.empty: No AI providers configured yet. Add one to enable the studio AI assistant — your key stays on this device.
ai.privacyBody: Keys are stored encrypted on this device only (AES-GCM in IndexedDB) and are never shown again after saving. Requests go directly from your browser to the provider — no proxy, no telemetry. Note: this protects keys at rest, not against a compromised device.

settings.placeholder: App preferences and AI provider keys (stored only on this device) will live here.

meta.title: laser-gen — 360° wrap art for laser engraving
meta.description: Design wrap art for cups, mugs and bottles in your browser: vector editor, live 3D preview, photo-to-engraving, AI assistant, LightBurn/xTool/LaserGRBL export. Free, open source, offline-capable.

hero.eyebrow: Free · Open source · 100% in your browser
hero.sub: A fully client-side PWA for laser engraving: draw, trace, or generate art, preview it live on a 3D vessel, and export millimeter-perfect files for your laser software.

features.title: Everything between photo and burn
features.subtitle: One tool for the whole wrap workflow — no installs, no accounts, no uploads.

wrap360.title: 360° wrap editor
wrap360.body: A millimeter-accurate artboard that maps the full circumference of your vessel, with seam and safe-zone guides.

preview3d.title: Live 3D preview
preview3d.body: See your design wrapped around the vessel in real time, with a laser-sweep shader showing exactly where the beam will travel.

photo.title: Photo to engraving
photo.body: Brightness, contrast and gamma plus Floyd–Steinberg, ordered dither, halftone and stipple — tuned per material.

vectorize.title: Vectorizer
vectorize.body: Trace raster images into clean vector paths right in the browser, with smoothing and simplification controls.

ai.title: AI assistant (BYOK)
ai.body: Prompt-to-SVG, prompt-to-image and a design copilot with your own Anthropic, OpenAI or OpenAI-compatible key. Keys never leave your device except to the provider you choose.

library.title: Library & jobs
library.body: Save projects and reusable assets locally, track burn attempts with power, speed and passes, and attach result photos.

export.title: Export for your laser
export.body: Physical-size SVG and DPI-correct PNG presets for LightBurn, xTool Creative Space and LaserGRBL, with rotary setup hints.

pick.title: Pick a vessel
pick.body: Choose a preset — Stanley Quencher, camp mug, wine tumbler and more — or measure your own.

design.title: Design or trace
design.body: Draw vectors, import SVGs, prep a photo, or let the AI assistant sketch something for you.

export.title: Export & burn
export.body: Download a millimeter-perfect file, set up your rotary, and watch it engrave.

vessels.title: Made for real vessels
vessels.subtitle: Built-in presets with real-world dimensions — the engrave window highlighted.
vessels.disclaimer: Preset dimensions are approximate community measurements, not manufacturer specs. Measure your own vessel before a production engraving.

openSource.title: Open source, MIT licensed
openSource.body: laser-gen is a community project. No backend, no telemetry, no paywall — audit every line, or help shape the roadmap.

footer.tagline: 360° wrap art for laser engraving, fully in your browser.
footer.mit: MIT licensed · Fully client-side · No telemetry

site.offline: You're offline — laser-gen keeps working; everything is stored on this device.

docsPages.title: Docs
docsPages.intro: Short, practical guides — from your first burn to adding your own AI provider.
docsPages.moreOnGithub: Full contributor documentation lives in the repo:

gettingStarted.title: Getting started
gettingStarted.description: The 60-second tour: pick a vessel, design, export, burn.

aiProviders.title: AI providers
aiProviders.description: Bring-your-own-key setup, privacy model and CORS notes.

vessels.title: Vessels
vessels.description: Built-in presets, their dimensions, and how to contribute one.

gettingStarted.intro: From zero to first burn in about five minutes — everything runs locally in your browser.
gettingStarted.quick1: Open the Studio and pick a vessel preset — or measure yours and enter the profile.
gettingStarted.quick2: Design on the unwrapped artboard: draw shapes, import an SVG, prep a photo, or ask the AI assistant.
gettingStarted.quick3: Check the live 3D preview — the wrap, seam and handle safe zones update as you edit.
gettingStarted.quick4: Export for your laser software, enter the object diameter in its rotary setup, and burn.
gettingStarted.wrapBody1: The artboard is the vessel's outer surface unrolled flat: its width equals the circumference at the widest engraved row, its height the engrave window. Anything you draw maps straight onto the vessel.
gettingStarted.wrapBody2: The left and right edges are the same line on the vessel — the seam. Elements that cross an edge wrap automatically, so continuous patterns stay continuous.
gettingStarted.diagramAlt: Diagram: a cylinder with its engrave band, unwrapped into a flat rectangle whose dashed edges mark the seam.
gettingStarted.offlineBody: laser-gen is an installable PWA: the whole app is cached on your device and every project is stored locally in IndexedDB. Design on the shop network — or with no network at all.

aiProviders.intro: The AI assistant is bring-your-own-key: your browser talks directly to the provider you configure.
aiProviders.model1: No backend, no proxy — requests go straight from your browser to the provider API.
aiProviders.model2: Keys are encrypted at rest (AES-GCM in IndexedDB) and never shown again after saving.
aiProviders.model3: A key is sent only to its own provider, over HTTPS, when you invoke an AI feature.
aiProviders.model4: Delete a provider in Settings and nothing retains it.
aiProviders.providersBody: One small interface with an adapter per provider; vendor SDKs load lazily, so they only cost bundle size when actually used.
aiProviders.rowOpenai: Chat (default gpt-4o-mini) plus image generation via gpt-image-1.
aiProviders.rowCustom: Ollama, LM Studio, OpenRouter, vLLM — any chat-completions endpoint.
aiProviders.corsBody: Because requests originate in the browser, the endpoint must allow cross-origin browser requests:
aiProviders.corsLocal: Local servers: start Ollama with OLLAMA_ORIGINS set (e.g. OLLAMA_ORIGINS="*" for local use) or your requests will be blocked.
aiProviders.corsHosted: Hosted gateways: OpenRouter and most hosted OpenAI-compatible endpoints send permissive CORS headers; Anthropic and OpenAI's official APIs accept browser requests with your own key.
aiProviders.securityBody: Encryption at rest protects keys on an intact device — it cannot protect against a compromised device. Use scoped keys where possible; see SECURITY.md in the repo for the full threat model.

vessels.intro: Presets spare you the calipers: pick a vessel and its profile drives both the 3D preview and the artboard.
vessels.measureBody: Presets are approximate community measurements, not manufacturer specs. For production work, measure height and diameter at a few points with a ruler or calipers — wall taper changes the circumference your art wraps across.
vessels.contributeBody: Measured a vessel we're missing? Presets are small profile definitions — see
vessels.modelCreditsBody: The camp mug and classic ceramic mug presets render community 3D models from Sketchfab, licensed CC-BY-4.0: “Stanley Mug” by Lime Zigubre and “Plain Mug” by LightSwitch. The same attribution appears next to the 3D preview when one of these vessels is active, and in the repository's NOTICE.md. All other vessels are parametric lathe profiles rendered by laser-gen itself.
